messages.content:
CVE-2020-13133 is a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Tufin SecureChange versions prior to R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1. This vulnerability, categorized under CWE-79, occurs when the software improperly neutralizes user-controllable input that is output as part of a web page. Successful exploitation requires administrative privileges to store the XSS payload, yet the payload can be triggered by unauthenticated users, potentially affecting all users accessing the affected service.

Rated as a MEDIUM severity issue, the impact of CVE-2020-13133 lies in its potential to allow attackers to execute arbitrary scripts in a user's browser, leading to session hijacking, redirection to malicious sites, or other malicious actions. This vulnerability is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1189, which involves drive-by compromise methods.

The affected versions of Tufin SecureChange include all iterations before R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1. Users are urged to upgrade to the fixed versions to mitigate the risk. Additionally, implementing recommendations such as M1050 (Data Backup), M1051 (Restrict Web-Based Content), M1048 (Application Isolation and Sandboxing), M1021 (Restrict Script Execution), and M1017 (User Training) can further enhance security.
